privacy.question: How does VexNexa handle privacy?
privacy.answer: We only scan publicly accessible content and don't store personal data. Our scans are fully GDPR compliant and we don't share data with third parties.

performance.question: Does scanning impact my website's performance?
performance.answer: No, our scans have no noticeable impact on your website. We simulate a normal visitor and don't perform stress tests. Scans run in the background without straining your site.

cookies.question: Do I need to accept cookies to use VexNexa?
cookies.answer: Basic functionality works without cookies. We only use cookies for analytics (optional) and to remember your preferences. You can refuse all cookies and the tool remains fully functional.

international.question: Can I scan international websites?
international.answer: Yes! VexNexa works with websites in all languages and countries. We support international character sets and can scan websites regardless of location or language of content.

account.question: Do I need an account or can I create teams?
account.answer: A free account is required to run scans. This lets us save your results, provide exports, and track improvements over time. No credit card needed to get started.

support.question: What kind of support do I get?
support.answer: All users get email support. We aim to respond within 1 business day. Business and Enterprise plans include priority support with faster response times.

q1.question: Can VexNexa guarantee legal compliance?
q1.answer: No tool can guarantee 100% legal compliance. VexNexa detects and reports accessibility issues, helps prioritize fixes, and supports WCAG and EAA readiness. Learn more about the [European Accessibility Act and what it means for agencies](/blog/what-is-the-european-accessibility-act). For legal risk assessment, consider combining automated scanning with expert review.

q2.question: Do I need an account to scan?

q3.question: Can I export branded reports for clients?
q3.answer: Yes. PDF and DOCX exports are available on paid plans. Business and Enterprise plans include white-label reports with your own logo, colours, and contact details.

q4.question: How does continuous monitoring work?
q4.answer: Schedule automatic scans daily, weekly, or monthly. VexNexa alerts you via email when scores drop or new critical issues appear. Track trends over time from your dashboard.

q5.question: Is VexNexa an accessibility overlay?
q5.answer: No. We scan your code structure and report real WCAG violations. We never inject widgets or scripts into your site. Our reports help developers fix issues at the source.

q6.question: What support is available?
q6.answer: All users get email support. We aim to respond within 1 business day. Business and Enterprise plans include priority support with faster response times.

q1.question: Can you guarantee legal compliance?
q1.answer: No tool can guarantee 100% legal compliance in all contexts. VexNexa detects and reports issues, assists remediation, and helps you adhere to WCAG and related standards. For legal risk assessment, consider an expert audit and ongoing governance process.

q2.question: How do page limits work?
q2.answer: Each plan includes a monthly page scan limit. A 'page' is one URL scanned. If you scan the same page multiple times in a month, each scan counts toward your limit. When you approach your limit, you'll receive a notification.

q3.question: What if I exceed my plan?
q3.answer: Free Plan: Service is blocked at limit. Upgrade to a paid plan to continue scanning. Paid plans: Service continues but you'll receive notifications to upgrade if you consistently exceed your limits.

q4.question: Do you support teams/SSO?
q4.answer: Pro plans support up to 5 team members. Business plans support up to 15 members with role-based access. SSO is available on Business and Enterprise plans. Contact us for Enterprise features.

q5.question: Can I export reports?
q5.answer: PDF export is available on all paid plans. Word export is available on Pro and Business plans. All exports include your scan results, prioritized issues, code snippets, and remediation recommendations.

q6.question: What's monitored continuously?
q6.answer: Continuous monitoring allows you to schedule automatic scans (daily, weekly, or monthly). You'll receive email alerts when scores drop or new issues are detected. This feature is available on Pro and Business plans.

q7.question: Do I need to schedule a demo?
q7.answer: No! VexNexa is 100% self-serve. Sign up, enter your URL, and get your accessibility report in minutes. We don't offer demos or sales calls.

q8.question: Is VexNexa an accessibility overlay?
q8.answer: No. We scan your code structure and give you WCAG violations to fix properly. We never inject widgets or scripts into your site. Overlays are controversial in the accessibility community—we analyze your actual code instead.

q9.question: Can VexNexa automatically fix issues?
q9.answer: No. Real accessibility requires proper code changes. We show you exactly what's wrong and how to fix it, but you need to make the changes yourself. Automated 'fixes' don't address root causes.

q10.question: Is there really a free plan?
q10.answer: Yes! Our free plan is forever free for 1 website and up to 100 pages per month. No credit card required. You get full WCAG 2.2 scanning, PDF reports, and 1 month of scan history. Upgrade only when you need more websites, pages, or advanced features like monitoring and Word exports.

scope.title: A Compliance Report, Not a Compliance Guarantee
scope.description: VexNexa reports document what automated testing detects. They cover a meaningful range of WCAG success criteria, but full conformance requires both automated and manual assessment. The report clearly marks which criteria were tested, which passed, and which need manual review.
scope.disclaimer: Use VexNexa reports as part of a broader compliance process — not as a standalone legal certification.

q1.question: What WCAG criteria does the compliance report cover?
q1.answer: The report covers testable WCAG 2.1 Level AA criteria and selected WCAG 2.2 criteria. The WCAG compliance matrix explicitly shows each criterion's status — pass, fail, needs manual review, or not tested — so there's no ambiguity about coverage.

q2.question: Can I customize the report before sharing it?
q2.answer: Yes. The DOCX export is fully editable — you can add commentary, remove sections, or paste content into your own templates. The PDF format is finalized for direct sharing or printing.

q3.question: Does the report include remediation guidance?
q3.answer: Each violation includes a description of the issue, why it matters, and a link to the relevant WCAG understanding document. The top priority fixes section ranks issues by impact to help teams plan their remediation sprints.

q4.question: How is this different from a basic accessibility checker output?
q4.answer: Basic checkers give you raw data — a list of violations and maybe a score. VexNexa compliance reports structure that data for decision-makers: executive summaries, severity breakdowns, WCAG matrices, and prioritized action lists. The report is designed to move projects forward, not just identify problems.

q5.question: Can I use these reports for legal compliance?
q5.answer: VexNexa reports provide strong evidence of accessibility testing and remediation planning. However, they should be used as part of a broader compliance strategy that includes manual testing and expert review. The reports clearly indicate which criteria were tested automatically versus which require manual assessment.

q6.question: Do reports include white-label branding?
q6.answer: Yes. Business and Enterprise plans include full white-label branding with your company logo, colors, and contact information. The reports appear as if generated by your own team, with no VexNexa branding visible.

hero.subtitle: Stop scrolling through unsorted error dumps. VexNexa checks your site for WCAG violations and shows you exactly which issues are critical, which are minor, and where each one lives in your HTML.

passFailProblem.title: Why "78% Accessible" Tells You Almost Nothing
passFailProblem.subtitle: A single percentage hides more than it reveals. Two sites can both score 78% — one has three critical keyboard traps, the other has forty missing alt texts on decorative images. The first is unusable. The second is mostly fine.
passFailProblem.solution: Basic accessibility checkers treat every failed rule equally. VexNexa doesn't. Every violation is weighted by real-world impact so you can separate the urgent from the cosmetic.

whatYouGet.subtitle: VexNexa goes beyond simple pass/fail testing. Get actionable insights that help you prioritize fixes and track improvement over time.

limitations.title: What Automated Checking Can't Do
limitations.description: No tool catches everything. Automated accessibility checkers reliably detect issues like missing alt text, color contrast failures, and missing form labels. But criteria that depend on content meaning, logical reading order, or complex keyboard interactions still need a human reviewer.
limitations.solution: VexNexa flags where manual review is recommended and helps you prioritize what automated testing does find — so your team spends expert time where it counts.

q1.question: How is VexNexa different from WAVE or Lighthouse?
q1.answer: WAVE and Lighthouse are excellent free tools for quick spot-checks. VexNexa adds severity ranking, element-level CSS selectors, exportable PDF/DOCX reports, score tracking over time, and white-label branding — features designed for teams that need to act on results, not just view them.

q2.question: Can I check more than one page at a time?
q2.answer: Yes. Paid plans support multi-page scans across your site. Each page produces its own severity-ranked violation list and contributes to an overall site health score.

q3.question: What accessibility standards does VexNexa check against?
q3.answer: VexNexa checks against WCAG 2.1 Level AA and selected WCAG 2.2 criteria using the axe-core engine. Each reported violation references the specific success criterion it relates to.

q4.question: Do I need to create an account?
q4.answer: You can run a single-page scan without an account to see the results. For ongoing monitoring, multiple-page scans, or to export reports, you'll need to create a free account.

q5.question: How accurate are the results?
q5.answer: VexNexa uses the axe-core engine — the same trusted engine used by Microsoft, Google, and the U.S. government. It reliably detects testable issues with high precision. However, automated testing can't catch everything — that's why VexNexa flags where manual review is recommended.

section2.paymentsNote: VexNexa does not store credit card or bank account numbers itself. All payment data is processed exclusively by our payment service provider Mollie B.V. within their PCI-DSS certified environment.
paymentsItems: We receive only a payment reference (payment ID), the status (success/failure), the amount, and the payment-method type (e.g. iDEAL, SEPA, card) from Mollie — never the card number itself.
For legally required invoicing we retain invoice details (company name, address, VAT number, invoice number, amount).
You can revoke a stored payment method at any time by canceling your subscription or contacting support.

section3.title: 3. Legal basis (Art. 6 GDPR)
items: Performance of contract – scans, results, account management.
Legitimate interest – security (rate-limiting, abuse detection), basic improvements.
Consent – analytics/marketing cookies and any opt-in communication.
Legal obligation – retention requirements and requests from authorities.

section4.title: 4. Cookies and tracking
section4.analyticsIntro: With consent, we may use privacy-friendly analytics (e.g., Vercel Analytics or alternative). We measure aggregated statistics, not individual profiles.
section4.manageText: You can manage consent through the
section4.utmText: We may temporarily store UTM parameters to understand origin. These contain no personal data.

section5.title: 5. How and why we use data
section5.noSelling: We do not sell or rent your data to third parties for marketing purposes.

section6.title: 6. Recipients and processors
section6.intro: We share data only with service providers that process it on our behalf under a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) compliant with Art. 28 GDPR. Per category, the current processors are:

processors.hostingValue: Vercel Inc. — application hosting and edge routing. Any data transfers to the United States are covered by EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and supplementary technical measures.
processors.databaseValue: Supabase (hosted on AWS in the EU, Frankfurt region) — primary storage for account and scan data. Data remains within the EU.
processors.authValue: Supabase Auth for email/password login, and Google LLC for the optional Google OAuth integration. With Google OAuth we receive only your email address, full name, and a Google account ID.
processors.paymentsValue: Mollie B.V. (Keizersgracht 313, 1016 EE Amsterdam) — handles all payments and is independently PCI-DSS certified. VexNexa does not store any card data.
processors.emailValue: Resend (Resend Inc.) — sends transactional email such as verification, password reset, and invoice/report notifications. Data transfers are covered by SCCs.

section7.title: 7. Transfers outside EU/EEA
section7.content: If transfers outside the EU take place (e.g., edge-routing or support logs), we use appropriate safeguards such as EU Standard Contractual Clauses and additional measures.

section8.title: 8. Data storage, security & retention
retentionItems: Scan data: 1 year (Free); longer for paid accounts (configurable in your account)
Account data: until account deletion or as long as legally required
Analytics: up to 24 months (aggregated/anonymized where possible)
Invoice data is retained for 7 years in accordance with the statutory tax retention obligation of the Dutch Tax Administration (Belastingdienst).
section8.retentionNote: We delete or anonymize earlier when data is no longer needed, unless retention is legally required (such as the fiscal retention obligation for invoice data).

section9.title: 9. Your rights (GDPR/AVG)
section9.accessDesc: Request what data we have about you.
section9.portabilityDesc: Receive data in a common format.
section9.objectionDesc: Object to processing based on legitimate interest.

section10.title: 10. Children
section10.content: Not directed at children under 16 years. Contact us if data has been collected; we will delete it.

section11.title: 11. Data breaches
section11.content: We immediately investigate the impact, limit risks and report if required to the Dutch Data Protection Authority and affected parties.

section12.title: 12. Automated decision-making
section12.content: We do not make decisions based solely on automated processing with legal effects for you.

section13.title: 13. Changes to this policy
section13.content: We communicate significant changes via email (if applicable), a notification on the site and update of the date.

section14.title: 14. Contact & complaints
section14.notSatisfied: Not satisfied? File a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority via

section4.title: 4. Payments & Subscriptions

The VAT amount is specified separately on your invoice.
trial: The free Trial remains free with fixed monthly limits (100 pages per month, 1 website)

paid: Prices include 21% VAT, unless explicitly stated otherwise for business customers.

Payments are processed exclusively through our payment provider Mollie B.V. (Keizersgracht 313, 1016 EE Amsterdam). Available methods include iDEAL, credit card, and SEPA direct debit. VexNexa does not store card or account details itself.

limits: Trial/Free: hard limit — service is blocked upon exceeding. An upgrade is required for continued access.

refunds: No refund for early cancellation unless legally required

section4.withdrawalIntro: For consumers within the EU, a statutory cooling-off period of 14 days generally applies to distance contracts (Book 6, Title 5, Section 2B BW). VexNexa is a digital service delivered immediately after starting a scan or confirming an upgrade.
section4.withdrawalWaiver: By starting a scan or confirming a (upgrade to a) paid subscription, you explicitly consent to immediate execution of the contract before the cooling-off period expires, and acknowledge that you thereby waive your right of withdrawal for the digital content already delivered for the relevant billing period (article 6:230p sub g BW).
section4.withdrawalFuture: For future billing periods, the right to cancel your subscription before the next renewal date remains, subject to the cancellation rules above. The waiver of the right of withdrawal applies only to the period already delivered.

privacyByDesign.content: We only scan publicly accessible content and do not attempt to access protected areas or extract personal information.

policy.title: What we publish
rules: We publish user-facing bugs that can affect scans, reports, login, billing or delivery.
We do not publish exploit details, customer data, credentials or internal infrastructure details.
For active incidents, updates include impact, status, workaround when available and the next expected update.
Major incidents may receive a short post-incident note after resolution.
